response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with roughly 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runner and assertion libraries, IDE components at National Instruments, CI/dev-ops infrastructure, MCP code generators), plus some mobile exposure via iOS and Apple TV work. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows neither: every role is an IC position, with no evidence of managing reports, running performance cycles, or leading organizational change. The gap is on the core axis of the job rather than the periphery, so an interview is unlikely to be productive for this opening.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, well beyond the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), IDE components, CI/CD and dev-ops setup, MCP code generators
Strong teaching and community inclination (workshops, Selenium courses, code katas, conference talks) that suggests mentoring potential
concerns: No people-management experience at all, against a hard requirement of 2+ years for this manager role
No formal technical-leadership role (team lead, tech lead, or org-level ownership) to satisfy the 3-year leadership requirement
Developer-tools work is spread across side projects and short stints rather than sustained ownership of build/test infrastructure at large-platform scale
